description: §61.2/§61.4 — a canonical valid `<endpoint>`: a required path=/method=/accepts=:enum, exhaustive per-variant arms (unit + two positional-payload variants), each returning an inline JSON-RPC value. The compile-shape proof that the declaration parses, accepts= resolves to the enum, the arms type-check + are exhaustive, and no E-ENDPOINT-* / E-TYPE-UNKNOWN-NAME fires.
rationale: §61.2/§61.4: a well-formed `<endpoint>` with a `:enum` accepts= and exhaustive arms is valid — none of the six E-ENDPOINT-* codes, E-TYPE-UNKNOWN-NAME, or E-TYPE-023 fire. JSON-RPC is a CONVENTION expressed by what each arm returns (§61.5), not a baked-in mode.
